title: ddRADSeq in a Field Setting
author: Mrinalini Watsa
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.y2efybe

steps.title: Resuspend P1 oligos to 100uM in 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend P1 oligos to 100uM in 0.5X AE Buffer (Qiagen), and follow instructions provided by supplier to do so if needed.

steps.title: In a PCR strip tube, mix...
steps.instruction: In a PCR strip tube, mix equal volumes of each oligo together to get a final concentration of 50uM of adapter. E.g. 20 µL of P1F_X and 20 µL l of P1R_X 11.

steps.title: In a MiniOne PCR System (PCR thermocycler, MiniOne, M4000, https://theminione
steps.instruction: In a MiniOne PCR System (PCR thermocycler, MiniOne, M4000, https://theminione.com/product/minione-pcr-system/ ) heat the mixture at 80 °C for 00:01:00

steps.title: Remove tube from the thermocylcer and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from the thermocylcer and place on a rack at 24 °C, or room temperature .

steps.title: Allow to cool to 24 °C,...
steps.instruction: Allow to cool to 24 °C, or room temperature for at least 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Further dilute the adapter to 5uM with 0
steps.instruction: Further dilute the adapter to 5uM with 0.5X Buffer AE Qiagen Catalog #19077 in 1.5ml tube. E.g Add 40 µL of annealed adapter and 360 µL 0.5X AE.

steps.title: Store adapters at 4 °C or...
steps.instruction: Store adapters at 4 °C or in freezer ( -20 °C, minimum ) for long term storage.

steps.title: 5In a PCR tube mix equal...
steps.instruction: 5In a PCR tube mix equal volumes of each oligo together to get a final concentration of 50uM of adapter. E.g. 50 µL of P2F_MspI and 50 µL of P2R_MspI.

steps.title: In a thermocylcer heat the mixture...
steps.instruction: In a thermocylcer heat the mixture at 80 °C for 00:01:00

steps.title: Remove tube from the thermocylcer and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from the thermocylcer and place on a rack at 24 °C, or room temperature .

steps.title: Allow to cool to 24 °C,...
steps.instruction: Allow to cool to 24 °C, or room temperature for at least 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Further dilute the adapter to 25uM with 0
steps.instruction: Further dilute the adapter to 25uM with 0.5 X Buffer AE Qiagen Catalog #19077 in 1.5ml tube. E.g Add 100 µL of annealed adapter and 100 µL 0.5X AE.

steps.title: Store adapter at 4 °C or...
steps.instruction: Store adapter at 4 °C or in freezer for long term storage.

steps.title: This can be done in a...
steps.instruction: This can be done in a strip of PCR tubes to allow you to use the multichannel pipette to dispense the P1 adapters. (You will need 20 tubes if using all the P1 adapters)

steps.title: Label tubes
steps.instruction: Label tubes.

steps.title: Add 19 µL of nuclease free water to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 19 µL of nuclease free water to each tube.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of P1 adapter...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of P1 adapter to corresponding one tube above.

steps.title: Repeat for all P1 adapters being used
steps.instruction: Repeat for all P1 adapters being used.

steps.title: Seal tubes, vortex briefly to mix, centrifuge
steps.instruction: Seal tubes, vortex briefly to mix, centrifuge.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of ddH2O to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of ddH2O to each tube.

steps.title: Rinse each sample in ddH2O by...
steps.instruction: Rinse each sample in ddH2O by vortexing three times for 00:00:05 each

steps.title: Using a sterile tip, transfer each...
steps.instruction: Using a sterile tip, transfer each disk to a pre-labeled 0.8 mL PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 45 µL to 50 µL...
steps.instruction: Add 45 µL to 50 µL of ddH2O so that it completely covers the disk

steps.title: Spin down tubes and make sure disk(s) are submerged
steps.instruction: Spin down tubes and make sure disk(s) are submerged

steps.title: Heat to 95 °C for 00:30:00 in a PCR machine
steps.instruction: Heat to 95 °C for 00:30:00 in a PCR machine

steps.title: Remove tube and pulse vortex or gently tap 60 times
steps.instruction: Remove tube and pulse vortex or gently tap 60 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:00:30
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:00:30

steps.title: Transfer all liquid into a pre-labelled...
steps.instruction: Transfer all liquid into a pre-labelled tube – this will be Elution A

steps.title: Add 30 µL of ddH 2...
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL of ddH 2 0 to the disk that remains in the PCR tube and repeat steps 5 - 10 to get Elution B

steps.title: Take 1 to 3 (2-3 mm...
steps.instruction: Take 1 to 3 (2-3 mm 3 ) punches from an FTA card using a fine scalpel or a hole punch, while maintaining sterility. Transfer it into a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
2.Rinse each sample in ddH2O by vortexing three times for five seconds each
3.Using a steril tip, transfer each disk to a pre-labeled PCR tube.
4.Add 45 to 50 uL of ddH2O (should cover the disk)
5.Spin down tubes and make sure disk is submerged
6.Heat to 95°C for 30 minutes in a PCR machine (
7.Remove tube and pulse vortex or gently tap 60 times.
8.Centrifuge for 30 seconds
9.Transfer all liquid into a pre-labelled tube – this will be Elution A
10.Add 45 to 50 uL (Confirm that you did use 30 ul for each elution) to the disk that remains in the PCR tube and repeat steps 4 – 9 to get Elution B
11.Store at 4°C if using it soon, or store at -20°C otherwise

steps.title: Store at 4 °C if using...
steps.instruction: Store at 4 °C if using it soon, or store at -20 °C for longterm storage.

steps.title: Vortex sample for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Vortex sample for 00:01:00

steps.title: Add 10 µL of Proteinase K
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of Proteinase K

steps.title: Incubate overnightat 65 °C - 70 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate overnightat 65 °C - 70 °C . The digest iscomplete when the colour of the supernatant has turned light orange

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Binding Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Binding Buffer to the tube containing 200 uL of Longmire's solution with blood.

steps.title: Pulse vortex for 00:00:20
steps.instruction: Pulse vortex for 00:00:20

steps.title: Incubate for 00:10:00 at 65 °C - 70 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:10:00 at 65 °C - 70 °C

steps.title: Add 500 µL of 96% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of 96% ethanol and pulse vortex 5 times

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16000 G for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16000 G for 00:02:00

steps.title: Carefully transfer as much supernatant as...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer as much supernatant as possible to the spin column, loading 500 µL at a time, centrifuge at 6000 G for 00:02:00 and discard the liquid.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 6000 G for 00:02:00 and discard the liquid
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 6000 G for 00:02:00 and discard the liquid.

steps.title: Add 720 µL of Wash Buffer,...
steps.instruction: Add 720 µL of Wash Buffer, centrifuge at 6000 G for 00:02:00 and discard the liquid.

steps.title: Centrifuge again at 10,000 G for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge again at 10,000 G for 00:04:00 and discard the liquid.

steps.title: Remove spin column and place into a labelled microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Remove spin column and place into a labelled microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Incubate tubes with caps off at...
steps.instruction: Incubate tubes with caps off at 56 °C to evaporate residual ethanol for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: During this incubation, place 2 mL,...
steps.instruction: During this incubation, place 2 mL, or sufficient amount of elution buffer into the bath to heat it.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of elution buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of elution buffer directly onto the membrane of each spin column.

steps.title: Incubate at roomtemperature for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at roomtemperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Repeat steps 28 to 30 with...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 28 to 30 with 30 µL of elution buffer for a second elution B. If you like, you can also elute this directly into Elution A for one tube at the end.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 G for 00:05:00 to collect eluted DNA
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 G for 00:05:00 to collect eluted DNA.

steps.title: Choose the OneDNA protocol on the Quantus fluorometer
steps.instruction: Choose the OneDNA protocol on the Quantus fluorometer.

steps.title: Calibrate the Quantus as follows
steps.instruction: Calibrate the Quantus as follows: a.Fill a Qubit assay tube with 200 µL dye and click ‘Blank’ (under ‘Calibrate).

steps.title: Prepare a labeled Qubit assay tube...
steps.instruction: Prepare a labeled Qubit assay tube for each sample to be quantified

steps.title: Add 200 µL of dye to each Qubit assay tube
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of dye to each Qubit assay tube. Critical Note: tubes containing dye need to be covered with aluminium foil to shield dye from light.

steps.title: Vortex the samples to mix them...
steps.instruction: Vortex the samples to mix them well and quickly spin down.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of each sample...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of each sample to each labeled Qubit assay tube.

steps.title: Vortex and spin assay tubes, and...
steps.instruction: Vortex and spin assay tubes, and incubate at 24 °C, or rtp for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Measure the sample in the fluorometer...
steps.instruction: Measure the sample in the fluorometer and record value in ng/uL. We have reused Quantus assay tubes after a recycling program. Rinse each tube with 10% bleach, two washes in distilled water, and 70% ethanol. Then autoclave. These tubes should be measured once without the sample, for some of them will have a very small amount of residual DNA. Then, add the sample, incubate for 5 minutes, and re-measure. Subtract the original reading from this one for an accurate reading. This method allows us to recylce the plastic tubes without detriment to our accuracy.

steps.title: b
steps.instruction: b.In a new Qubit assay tube, add 400 µL of dye and 2 µL of Lambda DNA, and click ‘Standard’. Then click 'Save'

steps.title: Prepare and label one PCR tube...
steps.instruction: Prepare and label one PCR tube for each sample to be amplified using the (NAME KIT)

steps.title: Add 9 µL of sample buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 9 µL of sample buffer (Green cap) to each tube.

steps.title: Dilute template DNA to 10 ng/uL
steps.instruction: Dilute template DNA to 10 ng/uL. Add 1 µL of this DNA to the PCR tube. If DNA concentration doesn't reach 10ng/uL, then add more. We added 3 µl of our template to each tube because our DNA concentration was too low.

steps.title: Pulse vortex the PCR tubes
steps.instruction: Pulse vortex the PCR tubes.

steps.title: Heat the samples at 95 °C...
steps.instruction: Heat the samples at 95 °C for 00:03:00 and then cool to 4 °C . Heating DNA for longer than 3 minutes at higher temperatures can cause damage to the DNA.

steps.title: While waiting for samples to cool, prepare a Master Mix
steps.instruction: While waiting for samples to cool, prepare a Master Mix: For each amplification reaction, combine 9 µL of Reaction Buffer (Blue cap) with 1 µL of Enzyme Buffer (Yellow cap). Prepare the Master Mix right before you use it and keep in on ice. If there is any left over after WGA, do not reuse it. Our enzyme:buffer concentration was 1:5.6, rather than 1:9 (by accident). This did not appear to have an effect on the amplification.

steps.title: Transfer 10 µL of the Master Mix to each sample
steps.instruction: Transfer 10 µL of the Master Mix to each sample.

steps.title: Incubate at 30 °C for 01:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 30 °C for 01:30:00 . If you had low concentration to start with, this can be extended for 30 more minutes.

steps.title: Inactivate samples at 65 °C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Inactivate samples at 65 °C for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Pause point
steps.instruction: Pause point: Samples can be stored at 4° C.

steps.title: Resuspend oligos to 100uM in 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend oligos to 100uM in 0.5X AE Buffer (Qiagen), using supplier's instructions if needed.

steps.title: In a PCR tube mix equal...
steps.instruction: In a PCR tube mix equal volumes of each oligo together to get a final concentration of 50uM of adapter. E.g. 20ul of P1F_X and 20ul of P1R_X 11.

steps.title: In a thermocylcer heat the mixture...
steps.instruction: In a thermocylcer heat the mixture at 80 °C for 00:01:00

steps.title: Remove tube from the thermocylcer and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from the thermocylcer and place on a rack at 24 °C, or rtp .

steps.title: Allow to cool to 24 °C,...
steps.instruction: Allow to cool to 24 °C, or rtp for at least 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Further dilute the adapter to 5uM with 0
steps.instruction: Further dilute the adapter to 5uM with 0.5 X AE in 1.5ml tube. E.g Add 40 µL of annealed adapter and 360 µL 0.5X AE.

steps.title: Store adapters at 4 °C or...
steps.instruction: Store adapters at 4 °C or in freezer for long term storage.

steps.title: Label tubes
steps.instruction: Label tubes. These dilutions can be done in a strip of PCR tubes to allow you to use the multichannel pipette to dispense the P1 adapters. (You will need 20 tubes if using all the P1 adapters)

steps.title: Add 19 µL of nuclease free water to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 19 µL of nuclease free water to each tube.

steps.title: 1Add 1 µL of P1 adapter...
steps.instruction: 1Add 1 µL of P1 adapter to corresponding one tube above.

steps.title: Repeat for all P1 adapters being used
steps.instruction: Repeat for all P1 adapters being used.

steps.title: Seal tubes, vortex briefly to mix, centrifuge
steps.instruction: Seal tubes, vortex briefly to mix, centrifuge.

steps.title: In a PCR tube mix equal...
steps.instruction: In a PCR tube mix equal volumes of each oligo together to get a final concentration of 50uM of adapter. E.g. 50ul of P2F_MspI and 50ul of P2R_MspI.

steps.title: In a thermocylcer heat the mixture...
steps.instruction: In a thermocylcer heat the mixture at 80 °C for 00:01:00

steps.title: Remove tube from the thermocylcer and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from the thermocylcer and place on a rack at 24 °C, or rtp

steps.title: Allow to cool to 24 °C,...
steps.instruction: Allow to cool to 24 °C, or rtp for at least 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Further dilute the adapter to 25uM with 0
steps.instruction: Further dilute the adapter to 25uM with 0.5 X AE in 1.5ml tube. E.g Add 100ul of annealed adapter and 100ul 0.5X AE.

steps.title: Store adapter at 4 °C or...
steps.instruction: Store adapter at 4 °C or in a freezer for long term storage.

steps.title: Choosing how to pool your samples...
steps.instruction: Choosing how to pool your samples is dependent on the number of samples involved and how successful you have been in each case in extracting DNA from the samples. Additionally, the number of dual indices you create will inform the maximum number of samples that can be pooled together. As such, there are a few general guidelines to follow: 1. Make sure you combine samples in the same pool that have roughly the same DNA output. i.e. pool all samples with the same concentration (as per your fluorometer reading) together. 2. When choosing indices, follow the barcoding system utilised in Thrasher et al. 2018 if you plan to do Illumina-platform sequencing (i.e. the MiSeq or HiSeq). If you are utilising a MinION or other 3rd gen sequencer with a higher error rate, demultiplexing your samples becomes very difficult if you use barcodes as short as the ones in Thrasher et al. 2018. We strongly encourage you to extend the length of barcodes to more easily distinguish samples from each other bioinformatically post sequencing.

steps.title: Once you have pooled your samples...
steps.instruction: Once you have pooled your samples together, you will need 20uL of your pools, containing ~ 400 ng of DNA each. If you do not have this concentration, but you have this amount of DNA in total, consider doing an SPRI bead cleanup to concentrate your DNA so you are left with this amount in the end. If you simply do not have enough DNA, consider a second extraction combined with this one via a SPRI bead cleanup to reach these targets. Without this much DNA, you might have a lower yield of reads, although it is not impossible to achieve desired results. We recommend a bare minimum of 100 ng of DNA for any single sample.

steps.title: Make a master mix of everything...
steps.instruction: Make a master mix of everything except the P1 adapters and DNA in a 2ml tube. Make the master mix for a few extra reactions so that you do not run out. Use the ligation mix table below. If you do not have those exact concentrations, then use the table from the next step instead: %s %s

steps.title: If you do not have those...
steps.instruction: If you do not have those exact concentrations, then use the table below: %s %s

steps.title: Vortex briefly to mix and centrifuge
steps.instruction: Vortex briefly to mix and centrifuge.

steps.title: Aliquot 19 ul of master mix...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 19 ul of master mix to each well of a PCR strip tube or plate (depending on the size of your reaction)

steps.title: Add 1ul of P1 adapter to...
steps.instruction: Add 1ul of P1 adapter to the corresponding well of the PCR plate (make sure that each pair of 2 digestion-ligation reactions receives a unique P1 adapter).

steps.title: Add 20ul of gDNA to each well.Note
steps.instruction: Add 20ul of gDNA to each well.Note: every well will have a different DNA sample.

steps.title: Use a pipette set to 30ul...
steps.instruction: Use a pipette set to 30ul and gently pipette up and down a few times to mix each reaction.

steps.title: Seal each PCR strip tube well,...
steps.instruction: Seal each PCR strip tube well, making sure the snap caps are down and that the contents of the tube are settled at the bottom.

steps.title: Incubate at the following temperatures and times
steps.instruction: Incubate at the following temperatures and times: 37 °C for 00:30:00 20 °C for 01:00:00 4 °C hold
1. Set a thick 1% agarose gel. For the MiniOne PCR system, we used ~19 mL agarose / gel + 1.5 uL dye, and the 6-toothed comb. Our gels could hold up to 35uL of product

steps.title: Critical Note
steps.instruction: Critical Note: Multiple samples can be run on the same gel, but to prevent contamination across lanes, only run samples that will be pooled into the same pool on the same gel. We additionally reused 1x TB buffer for gels containing samples from a common pool. Load your samples into wells along with a 100bp ladder. Samples may have to be loaded into multiple wells.

steps.title: We ran gels for ~25 minutes...
steps.instruction: We ran gels for ~25 minutes until the 1000 bp band could be clearly distinguished from the 1500 bp band on the ladder. Run until the desired size can be clearly distinguished from the ladder.

steps.title: Take and label photo of gel for future reference
steps.instruction: Take and label photo of gel for future reference.

steps.title: The following steps are based on...
steps.instruction: The following steps are based on the gel-extraction kit you use. We used this kit Wizard SV Gel and PCR Clean-Up System Promega Catalog #A9281

steps.title: Prepare an empty 1
steps.instruction: Prepare an empty 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube and record its weight.

steps.title: Using one gel excision tip, cut...
steps.instruction: Using one gel excision tip, cut out the region surrounding desired size as cleanly as possible for each lane and combine all gel slices in the microcentrifuge tube. Take and label photo of excised gel for future reference. For all samples, we excised the region surrounding the 500 bp and the 1000 bp band.

steps.title: Re-weigh microcentrifuge tube and record
steps.instruction: Re-weigh microcentrifuge tube and record.

steps.title: go to step #85
steps.instruction: go to step #85

steps.title: Add Membrane Binding Solution at a...
steps.instruction: Add Membrane Binding Solution at a ratio of 10μl of solution per 10mg of agarose gel slice. Vortex the mixture

steps.title: Incubate at 56 °C for 00:10:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56 °C for 00:10:00 or until the gel slice is completely dissolved. Note: Vortex the tube every few minutes to increase the rate of agarose gel melting.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube briefly at room...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube briefly at room temperature to ensure the contents are at the bottom of the tube. Note: Once the agarose gel is melted, the gel will not resolidify at room temperature.

steps.title: Place one SV Minicolumn in a...
steps.instruction: Place one SV Minicolumn in a Collection Tube for each dissolved gel slice.

steps.title: Transfer the dissolved gel mixture to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the dissolved gel mixture to the SV Minicolumn assembly and incubate for 00:01:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge the SV Minicolumn assembly in...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the SV Minicolumn assembly in a microcentrifuge at 16,000 × g (14,000 rpm) for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Remove the SV Minicolumn from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the SV Minicolumn from the Spin Column assembly, and discard the liquid in the Collection Tube.

steps.title: Return the SV Minicolumn to the Collection Tube
steps.instruction: Return the SV Minicolumn to the Collection Tube.

steps.title: Wash the column by adding 700...
steps.instruction: Wash the column by adding 700 µL of Membrane Wash Solution to the SV Minicolumn.

steps.title: Centrifuge the SV Minicolumn assembly for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the SV Minicolumn assembly for 00:01:00 at 16,000 × g (14,000rpm).

steps.title: Empty the Collection Tube as before,...
steps.instruction: Empty the Collection Tube as before, and place the SV Minicolumn back in the Collection Tube.

steps.title: Repeat the wash with 500 µL...
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash with 500 µL of Membrane Wash Solution, and centrifuge the SV Minicolumn assembly for 00:05:00 at 16,000 × g.

steps.title: Remove the SV Minicolumn assembly from...
steps.instruction: Remove the SV Minicolumn assembly from the centrifuge, being careful not to wet the bottom of the column with the flowthrough.

steps.title: Empty the Collection Tube, and recentrifuge...
steps.instruction: Empty the Collection Tube, and recentrifuge the column assembly for 00:01:00 with the microcentrifuge lid open (or off) to allow evaporation of any residual ethanol.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the SV Minicolumn to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the SV Minicolumn to a clean 1.5ml microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Apply 20 µL of nuclease-free water...
steps.instruction: Apply 20 µL of nuclease-free water directly to the center of the column without touching the membrane with the pipette tip. Alternatives: You can use an elution buffer of your choice here instea of nuclease-free water, depending on your downstream applications

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16,000 × g (14,000 rpm)
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16,000 × g (14,000 rpm).

steps.title: Remove the eluted volume and place...
steps.instruction: Remove the eluted volume and place it back on the spin column.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16,000 ×...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16,000 × g (14,000 rpm) to concentrate your DNA.

steps.title: Discard the SV Minicolumn, and store...
steps.instruction: Discard the SV Minicolumn, and store the microcentrifuge tube containing the eluted DNA at 4 °C or -20 °C . The volume of the eluted DNA should be approximately 18-19 μl.
